problem.title: Insert Interval
problem.description: Given a set of non-overlapping intervals, insert a new interval into the intervals (merge if necessary).
assumptions: Existing intervals are non-overlapping and sorted.

example.explanation: [2,5] overlaps [1,3], merge to [1,5]. Then [6,9] doesn't overlap.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Linear Scan
solutions.description: Make a single left-to-right pass through the sorted intervals in three phases: first copy all intervals that end before the new interval starts, then greedily merge all intervals that overlap the new interval by expanding its bounds, and finally copy the remaining non-overlapping intervals. The key insight is that because the original list is already sorted and non-overlapping, each phase needs only a simple boundary comparison and at most one merged interval is ever produced.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def insert(intervals: List[List[int]], new_interval: List[int]) -> List[List[int]]:
    result: List[List[int]] = []
    i = 0
    n = len(intervals)

    # Add intervals that come entirely before new_interval
    while i < n and intervals[i][1] < new_interval[0]:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1

    # Merge overlapping intervals
    while i < n and intervals[i][0] <= new_interval[1]:
        new_interval[0] = min(new_interval[0], intervals[i][0])
        new_interval[1] = max(new_interval[1], intervals[i][1])
        i += 1
    result.append(new_interval)

    # Add remaining intervals
    while i < n:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1

    return result
